company: Global Innovation Exchange, University of Washington
description: Provide L1-L3 customer-facing IT support; analyze data from incidents, logs, and user feedback to identify root causes and recurring patterns, coordinate resources across teams, and propose data-driven improvements. Maintain A/V infrastructure and documentation while resolving 15+ JIRA tickets weekly.

company: Tulibot (AI Captioning Smart Glasses for Deaf)
description: Owned end-to-end development for an assistive tech platform; defined product strategy and roadmap, conducted research and Market Analysis. Shipped features that reached 5,000+ users in 6 months with 15% MoM retention; used analytics and user feedback to measure product performance.

Led the full product development lifecycle and SDLC; translated stakeholder needs into user-centered requirements and user stories. Tracked performance metrics and user feedback to scale the platform to 2,000+ users. Directed technical strategy and operations, delivering cost-effective, trustworthy solutions.

Designed and developed high-throughput microservices and distributed systems (Go, gRPC, Kafka) processing 10M+ daily updates with 99% uptime. Drove continuous system enhancement and managed CI/CD pipelines (Kubernetes, ArgoCD), reducing deployment failures by 20%.

Designed the next generation Online Intelligence service for EUREKA Omni with management consulting approach. Built Issue Tree using DSPS framework; interviewed customer representatives; created prioritized features roadmap for chosen industry.

Delivered presentations on building developer communities through hands-on workshops. Hosted Cloud Study Jam for 30 participants. Taught 25+ students Anaconda & OpenCV for ML projects. Organized 5 weekly classes (AI, IoT, Cloud, Web, Mobile).

Coordinated resources with a co-lead from Informatics. Defined committee roles, executed event preparation end-to-end with risk management. Enabled 35 teams to successfully register through improved processes and communication.
Built a visual remote-sensing prototype to capture nutrition information and stem size. Developed an end-to-end communication pipeline from microcontroller to backend server (MERN).
Collected and labeled ball-sensing data for a detection algorithm on a 3-wheeled soccer robot. Built the visual navigation system (ball-tracking algorithm, detection, vision-based localization).
Implemented Cisco Small Cell solutions (WLC 2504, Aironet 3700, USC Controller 8088, VMware ESXi). Performed stress-traffic testing and RFC2544 testing to validate reliability.
